title: arenaria armerina var. pseudarmeriastrum
intro:
Arenaria armerina var. pseudarmeriastrum, commonly known as a variety of the Mountain Sandwort, is a plant species that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The therapeutic actions of this plant include its ability to exhibit antimicrobial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, making it a potential treatment for various infections. The bioactive constituents of Arenaria armerina var. pseudarmeriastrum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

However, I couldn't verify the full extent of the health benefits, therapeutic actions, and bioactive constituents of this plant.
side_effects: Arenaria armerina var. pseudarmeriastrum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, as well as allergic reactions like skin irritation and respiratory problems. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also lead to diarrhea, abdominal cramps, and potentially, kidney damage. The plant's essential oils may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and exacerbate underlying health conditions. Furthermore, improper use of the plant may cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue in some individuals.
